title: Changes to Bank of Canada Governing Council
summary: The Bank of Canada announced the departure of Deputy Governor Rhys Mendes on April 10, 2026, and the retirement of Deputy Governor Sharon Kozicki on July 15, 2026. This represents two significant personnel changes within the Governing Council in a short timeframe.
key_points: Deputy Governor Rhys Mendes departing April 10, 2026
Deputy Governor Sharon Kozicki retiring July 15, 2026
Both are Governing Council members at the Bank of Canada
why_it_matters: Personnel changes at major central bank leadership can signal potential shifts in monetary policy stance or communication approach. Two deputy governors departing within months of each other increases uncertainty about the Bank of Canada's policy trajectory.
macro_context: Bank of Canada Governing Council manages Canada's monetary policy. Deputy governors participate in interest rate decisions. Their departures could affect policy continuity.
